name: GT-bh-cstar
source: HOS, refs/hos/joa-m.md:458 (1.3.2), VERBATIM: "$\mathscr{A}$  is called a Banach algebra if  $\|xy\| \le \|x\| \|y\|$  for all  $x, y \in \mathscr{A}$ ." and "the norm satisfies  $\|x^*x\| = \|x\|^2$  for all  $x \in \mathscr{A}$  then  $\mathscr{A}$  is said to be a  $C^*$  algebra" and "B(H), the bounded linear operators on H, is a  $C^*$  algebra in the usual operator norm". Hence in B(H): submultiplicativity ||xy||<=||x|| ||y||, and ||r^2||=||r^*r||=||r||^2 for r=r^*.
